subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Check if Node, npm, and Python are installed, preparing for Claude Code installation in the next step

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source LLM resources, with personal usage experience and insights

Proficient in classic NLP deep learning models (Transformer, LLaMA, GLM series) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs); master at least one common deep learning framework like PyTorch; maintain interest in the working principles and latest developments of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) since GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; knowledgeable in NLP fundamentals and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation); skilled in common NLP libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Description: Install Node, npm, and Python via command line.
Note: Output may be similar to "v22.21.1"
Note: Output may be similar to "10.9.4"
Note: Output may be similar to "Python 3.11.13"

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Environment configuration resources ready
Output Description: Server configured with Python and Node environments, meeting model deployment requirements

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Install and configure Claude Code via Node

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Install and Configure Claude Code
Input Description: Install and configure Claude Code based on Node environment
# Configure environment variables (using ~/.bashrc as example, similar for ~/.zshrc etc.)
# Verify successful startup, enter claude command line, type any text and receive response without errors

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Available Claude Code service
Output Description: Generate target website PRD files through Claude Code

content: Build tavily-mcp service with web search, content reading, and intelligent Q&A capabilities

subsections.title: Participants

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Tavily MCP Service Build and Startup

Using remote MCP server URL with Tavily API key:
Run the following to verify connection:

content: Output Name: Available tavily web search MCP service

steps.title: Metaso MCP Service Build and Startup

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build metaso-mcp service with web search, content reading, and intelligent Q&A capabilities

subsections.title: Participants
content: Skills Required: Familiar with common Linux commands

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Metaso MCP Service Build and Startup
Input Description: Install and start related MCP services using remote MCP server URL
claude mcp add -s user -t http search-metaso https://metaso.cn/api/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer xxx" # Replace xxx with API key
Run the following to verify connection:

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Available metaso web search MCP service
Output Description: Build and start metaso-mcp service

steps.title: GitHub, HuggingFace, arXiv MCP Service Build and Startup (Optional)

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build github-mcp service with GitHub search and content reading capabilities
Build huggingface-mcp service with HuggingFace search and content reading capabilities
Build arxiv-mcp service with arXiv search and content reading capabilities

subsections.title: Participants
content: Skills Required: Familiar with common Linux commands and Python

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: GitHub, HuggingFace, arXiv MCP Service Build and Startup (Optional)
Input Description: Install and start related MCP services by installing Python dependencies
Note: Success indicator: "Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:PORT (Press CTRL+C to quit)"
claude mcp add -s user -t sse paper-arxiv http://127.0.0.1:4002/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse code-github http://127.0.0.1:4001/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse model-huggingface http://127.0.0.1:4003/sse

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Name: Available MCP services for GitHub, HuggingFace, and arXiv
Output Description: Build and start github-mcp, huggingface-mcp, arxiv-mcp services

steps.title: Generate Research Reports Using Claude Code

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Claude Code to automatically call the previous MCP services to generate research reports

subsections.title: Participants
content: Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with cutting-edge and popular open/closed-source LLM resources, with personal usage experience and insights

Proficient in classic NLP deep learning models (Transformer, LLaMA, GLM series) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs); master at least one common deep learning framework like PyTorch; maintain interest in the working principles and latest developments of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) since GPT-3.5

Proficient in Python, basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; knowledgeable in NLP fundamentals and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation); skilled in common NLP libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba)
Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Step Input
content: Input Name: Generate Research Reports Using Claude Code
Input Description: Interact with Claude Code to confirm input information for generating research reports
Provide existing research report template.md, research report generation workflow document skill.md, and flowchart.md
skill.md # Research report generation workflow document (required)
full_research_report_workflow.md # Workflow diagram for document generation (required)
Start Claude Code in project root to generate research report:
# Start claude, ensure no other files except files/, skill.md, and flowchart in project root
> Generate Deerflow research report based on skill.md
Note: There will be options requiring user confirmation during the process, then the final research report will be automatically generated

subsections.title: Step Output
content: Output Description: Claude Code automatically generates the final research report
